articles.description: BBC golf correspondent Iain Carter assesses where Tiger Woods' latest arrest leaves his legacy.
articles.title: Tiger Woods: Where does latest arrest leave golf great's legacy?

articles.description: Watch Sportscene coverage, listen to Sportsound and follow live text commentary, score updates and match report from Scotland vs Japan, a World Cup warm-up match.
articles.title: Scotland vs Japan: Watch World Cup warm-up, listen to commentary & follow live text

articles.description: Red Bull's Max Verstappen and Formula 1's bosses have plenty to ponder after Japanese Grand Prix qualifying.
articles.title: Japanese Grand Prix: Max Verstappen has 'a lot to figure out' after qualifying 11th at Suzuka

articles.description: Senegal parade the Africa Cup of Nations trophy before Saturday's friendly match against Peru in Paris - despite being stripped of the title earlier this month.
articles.title: Senegal parade Afcon trophy before Peru friendly despite being stripped of title by Caf

articles.description: Jordan Henderson says England players will support Ben White after the Arsenal defender was booed by fans at Wembley in Friday's 1-1 draw with Uruguay.
articles.title: Jordan Henderson: Ben White will have support of England players

articles.description: British rider Tom Pidcock crashed into a ravine on Friday and will miss the remainder of the race.
articles.title: Tom Pidcock forced out of Tour of Catalunya after 'horror' fall

articles.description: Vivianne Miedema's double helps Manchester City edge closer to the Women's Super League title with a comfortable derby win over neighbours United.
articles.title: Manchester United 0-3 Manchester City: Vivianne Miedema scores twice

articles.description: World number two Jannik Sinner reaches a fourth Miami Open final thanks to a straight-sets win over Alexander Zverev.
articles.title: Miami Open: Jannik Sinner beats Alexander Zverev to reach final again

articles.description: Kirsty Muir becomes the first British woman to win both the slopestyle and overall World Cup titles by finishing second in the season finale in Switzerland.
articles.title: Kirsty Muir: British freestyle skier wins historic World Cup titles

articles.description: Great Britain's Georgia Hunter Bell is beaten in the 1500m in Melbourne in her first race since she was crowned women's world indoor champion.
articles.title: Georgia Hunter Bell beaten by Australian Claudia Hollingsworth in Melbourne
